## shape.md

<!-- role: data -->
# Shape

The shape body.


## User-Provided Spec Draft

<!-- role: data -->
## User Spec

User-authored draft.


## Authority of User Input

The user has provided an existing spec draft (above) as authoritative source material. Treat it as higher priority than the specialist proposals: preserve the user's detail, features, acceptance criteria, constraints, and taste preferences wherever they do not contradict shape.md. Use the specialist proposals to sharpen, enrich, and fill gaps — do not replace the user's draft with a rewrite. If specialists and the user draft disagree on a non-conflicting detail, favor the user draft.

## Specialist Proposals

<!-- role: data -->
### Feature Specialist Proposal

Feature-focused proposal markdown.

---

### Constraints Specialist Proposal

Constraints-focused proposal markdown.

---


## Output Directory

Write spec.md, constraints.md, and optionally taste.md to: /tmp/build/
Use the Write tool to create each file.

## Gap Flagging

When writing each output file, append a final section titled `## Inferred / Gaps`. Under that heading list every load-bearing fact in the file you inferred without the user's input or shape.md directly stating it. Use one bullet per item: `- <fact> — inferred because: <one-line reason>`. If every load-bearing fact is source-backed, write `(none)`. The user will edit this section to confirm or override your guesses before plan runs.
